item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got "Included page "component:black-highlighter-theme-dev" does not exist". Might wanna fix that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To answer your first question: The O5-10 didn't know Everwoods was an SCP-4224 instance at the time, so he had no reason to suspect her. Since the Drone pathogen is invisible he wouldn't know he was infected until it's too late (and neither would the guards).

Your second question is explained right at the start of the article, with the paragraph explaining how DR-O1 works:

After initial infection, the disease begins the transformation in the prefrontal cortex and progresses posteriorly. Meaning that personality is affected first, before affecting other areas such as facial recognition, memory, language, reading, etc.

i.e. the O5 gained Everwoods's personality and motives before his memories were overridden.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just one question: How did Everwoods manage to "drone-ify" O5-10 at the end? Yeah, I know they intended to "see her off in person", but as an O5 they have to be accompanied by personal guards from the O5 Council's personal MTF (the Red Right Hand), right? And there's also this one question raised by WrongJohnSilver on a different thread:

Why would O5-10 retain knowledge of the password for the system?

That's a very crucial plot hole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and removed the O5 Council?

"Tried to remove the O5 Council" is what I think happened, if the "NULL ERROR: STRING CANNOT BE EMPTY" message is any indication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incredibly well-executed.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait… If I'm correct, Everwood accessed the terminal impersonating O5-10, and started the Ennui Protocol, making himself the Administrator (who is some random hash) and removed the O5 Council?

Sneaky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tasha is not a real legal name, you probably want Natalya.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was not my intention. Thank you for pointing it out! I've changed the name to "Tasha Ignatova", please let me know if this is also incorrect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ilya Ignatov is a strictly male name. Are they intended to be transgender? If so, this needs to actually be explicitly mentioned in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a good point, 5 times the calories than normal is a lot.
I wouldn't say it would cause a famine, but in certain places im sure such intake cannot be afforded, probably ending in death instead of famine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting.

I'll say its a slow burn to get to the point where we see the historical connection, but the unraveling of Dr. Selks and the record of altered historical events really intrigued me as a reader. If anything, I would have loved to have seen more reversions of history and the logic behind them, since your presentation here is quite good.

That said, this is not without problems. The way you play the O5s here is a little close to the ground. These are supposed to be the top dogs of the Foundation, and you have them overseeing disputes between Level 3 staff. Shouldn't there be Level 4 Site Directors and such handling this minutia? Additionally, the interview logs between Everwood and 4000 didn't really grab me, as they seemed like the typical "Humanoid skip gets in a researcher's head and causes problem" series of events, and are easily the weakest part of the article for me.

Overall, +1. There are some dings and dents, but through and through a solid contest entry. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dig the formatting, as well as when the guy got locked out for trying to take over the foundation.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hold on, wouldn't spreading DR-01 globally cause a famine? like suddenly everyone'd have to eat three times (by average) the usual amount they do for five days.

item.title: There's a mistake here
item.content:encoded: Ilya Ignatov is a male name. Ilya is a strictly male name and Ignatov is a male version of this surname. I suggest changing the first name and if you want to keep the surname, changing it to Ignatova which is a female version of this surname.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Selks: What a long-winded explanation.

If you feel the need to have an in-narrative comment about how much the writing drags on with no point, it's probably a good sign that the writing drags on with no point.

The dialogue in this felt really overwritten forced, and cliched. None of the characters felt engaging outside of the role they played in the story. And what bugged be most is that the anomaly felt completely unrelated to the story that this article was trying to tell- a mortal sin in SCP writing. If the beginning of the article feels completelt irrelevant to the theme and to the end, how can it be called a complete or satisfying story? This whole article just felt like a mess that never came together coherently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: History will bee kind to me

item.title: Love it +1
item.content:encoded:
The SCPs I tend to be most found to are the ones that want to tell a story aside from just describing an anomaly. Bonus points for a character based story. Thanks for all the Selks and Everwood chat the reseracher's character development was a lot stronger.
I didnt read that many 4000's entriesn but this one is my favorite for now. Congrats

PD: the name is what grabbed my attention at first, then the formatting and then the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the pun, but I tend to like puns. I'm going to make sure and save this version in case people don't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I took it down due to a (relatively) mixed reception. Lots of people liked the concept but felt the article was hollow, making up for its shortcomings with pretty lights. I attempted a rewrite, and I'm not sure if I managed to fix that issue, but it's back.

This was originally called Gaslight, but many missed the pun. I changed it to something more relevant.

And thank you! The CSS theme is heavily inspired by djkaktus' darkbody theme, but the terminal CSS is my own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am glad to see this up again, why was it taken down? Also, was there an alternate title to this originally? Easily the best looking entry I mean wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for pointing those out! Funny thing is, I was actually trying to think of the word "obsolescence", but my 3AM brain just wouldn't find it so I settled for "redundancy" instead.

All of the errors you mentioned have been corrected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have definitely improved this. The interplay between Selks and Everwood really gets her character across, and the conversation with O5-10 especially shows that it's not just a case of the big mean Head Researcher picking on the poor defenseless Level 3. He's a complete shitter, but she's nuanced and unscrupulous in other ways. I can believe now that she would try and promote herself to Administrator.

+ That said, found a bunch of typos, all of them in offset 3.
– hide block

offset 3

Please place look directly at the screen.

the free, wireless power transmission

don't need 'the'

Perl Harbor

I guess this could be intentional?

and was dismantled

Missing 'it'.

merely an inside joke

Missing 'to'.

anomalous items; even more so

That should be a comma.

I don't think it's so much redundancy of amnestics as obsolescence, or just total failure.

item.title: ... For I Intend To Write It
item.content:encoded:
With the 4k contest coming to a close, I'm putting this back up. I'm not totally happy with it, and I'll probably rewrite it again once the contest is over.

Original Critters: Uncle Nicolini, IndigoElks, Geckoguy2704, incidental_cabbage, WrongJohnSilver & magnadeus!

Rewrite Critters: MrBazzle, Vivax & Probably more who I'm forgetting!

Huge props to MrPines for the awesome hover code & Rounderhouse for the improved CSS!

[+] Image Attributions
[-] Image Attribution

warning.png:
http://scp-wiki.wdfiles.com/local--files/the-great-hippo/scp_trans.png (modified)

factory.jpg:
http://www.geograph.org.uk/photo/1733287 (modified)

ironlung.jpg:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Polmone_d%27acciaio_(Alvis_Motor_Company)_.jpg (modified)
